flashcards.definition: A key chemistry concept about organic compounds.
A saturated hydrocarbon with only single C-C bonds. General formula: CₙH₂ₙ₊₂. Examples: methane (CH₄), ethane (C₂H₆), propane (C₃H₈).
A specific group of atoms in an organic molecule responsible for its characteristic chemical reactions (e.g., -OH = alcohol, -COOH = carboxylic acid).
A large macromolecule made of many repeating monomer units linked by covalent bonds. Examples: polyethylene, proteins, DNA.
Compounds with the same molecular formula but different structural arrangements, resulting in different properties.
